title: CRISPR and Genome Editing: From Molecular Memory to Deliberate Change
description: This is a demonstration course that shows what Coursewerk can produce; it is not a course package intended for direct use in real teaching. The two-chapter introductory biology demo first treats CRISPR as an evolved microbial defense system built from molecular memory, RNA-guided recognition, and nuclease action. It then follows the engineering workflow from target design and DNA cleavage through repair outcomes, delivery, validation, off-target uncertainty, applications, and responsible decision-making. Each chapter includes a concept map, accessible study guide, visual slide deck, assessment-design guide, and worked practice set. The package was produced automatically by an AI agent following Coursewerk from version-pinned Wikipedia pages and retained rights evidence, without human-authored lesson content or subject-matter editing; human involvement was limited to commissioning, pipeline audit, visual review, and publication oversight.

chapters.title: CRISPR as a Biological System: Memory, Targeting, and Cas9
Editing Genomes: Repair Outcomes, Delivery, Limits, and Decisions
